sutta: 60 6 the variable sariputta said herein among who has achieved virtue has achieved concentration achieved wisdom may both completely enter the ending of perception and feeling and may emerge there from this is so if here in this life he make not the gain of knowledge he will surely go beyond the deva community that feed on solid food and arise in the mind-created body provided he enter and emerge from the ending of perception and feeling this is so i just stopped here for a while here the verbal sarikuta is talking about the ending of perception and feeling this is a statement it's a very high state of samadhi and very few people are supposed to be able to to have this attainment only anagamis and arahants are supposed to be able to enter the ending of perception and feeling when perception and feeling stops her then the normal sex consciousness also stops them so that's why this day is called niroda samapati attainment of cessation and here sarikuta is saying that in this this person when he enters the the ending of perception and feeling and emerge if if he does not make the gain of knowledge in this very life and that means if he does not become an arghandla he will go beyond the deva community that feed on solid food and arise in a mind-created body this is what our verbal sarikuta says [Music] where it was mentioned that an anagami can be reborn in the in the plane the formless realm uh realm of neither perception or non-perception was mentioned then so probably um this might refer to them when he had thus spoken the vulnerable udain said to the verbal sariputta this is not so variable sariputta now happens it that should among enter and emerge from the ending of perception and feeling he will surely go beyond the deva community that feed on solid food and arise in the mind pictured body it is not so the second time the variables sarikuta spoke in like manner to the mounts and the second time the variable udain contradicted him as before the third time the rambo sarikuta spoke in light manner to the monks and the third time the verbal udain contradicted him then the remember sariputta thought even unto a third time the verbal udain contradicts me and no monk supports me what if i were to go to the exalted 1 and he went to where the exalted 1 was and saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated the verbal sariputta addressed the monks speaking even as before and he repeated exactly what he mentioned before when he had thus spoken the verbal udain replied this is not so verbal sarikutta or does it happen that if a monk enter and emerge from the ending of perception and feeling he will surely go beyond the deva community that feed on solid food and arise in a mind-created body it is not so and the second time the verbal sariputta spoken in the same manner to the monks the second time the verbal udine contradicted him third time the rainbow sarikuta spoke in the same way to the monks and the third time the verbal udine contradicted him as before then the verbal sariputta thought again verily before the face of the exalted 1 even unto a third time the vulnerable udain contradicts me and no monk supports me i had best be silent and so the wearable sarikuta was silent then the exalted 1 addressed the verbal udain and said but who do you hold who dying has a mind created body and he replied those devas lord who are formless perception made [Music] and the buddha said why do you think udain that the word of a witness fool like you is just the thing to declare and just stop here for a while this is 1 of the sutas where you find the buddha sometimes calls the monks calling him a witness fool here it's not that the buddha is so good now that he doesn't score he's appropriate to school and he will school then this continues [Music] then the exalted 1 addressed the verbal ananda thus is it possible and undone that you can look on with indifference at an elder monk being vexed thoroughly ananda compassion grows not from allowing an elder monk to be vexed then the exalted 1 addressed the monk saying here in monks among who has achieved virtue achieve concentration achieved wisdom may both completely enter the ending of perception and feeling and may emerge there from this is so if here in this life he make not the gain of knowledge he will verily go beyond the deva community that feed on solid food and arise in a mind-created body provided he enter and emerge from the ending of perception and feeling this is so thus spoke the exalted 1 and when he had thus spoken the well gone 1 arose and entered the dwelling now not long after the the departure of the exalted 1 the verbal ananda went up to the vulnerable uppavana and said to him just now whenever upavana some monks were vexing the elder monk and we never protested therefore wonder not verbal sir should the exalted 1 after coming from seclusion bring the matter up and relate the whole affair to the vulnerable uppavana already even fearfulness has come upon us and in the evening after coming out from exclusion the exalted 1 went to the service hall and he sat down on the sea that was ready so seated he said to the vulnerable upavana having how many qualities supervana does an elder among his fellows in the holy life become pious loved respected and 1 he ought to become lord having 5 qualities an elder becomes pious love respected and 1 he ought to become what 5 lord herein he is virtuous he is learned he has a pleasant voice a good enunciation [Music] at will he obtains to the 4 states of jannah and by destroying the asavas he enters and abides in the liberation by mind deliberation by wisdom fairly lord having these 5 qualities an elder among his fellows in the holy life becomes pious loved respected and what he ought to become well said well said upavana it is even as you say if these 5 qualities are not completely found in an elder will his fellows in the holy life respect honor reverence and venerate him for his broken teeth his gray hairs his wrinkled skin but verily upavana and these 5 things are found in an elder then his fellows in the holy life respect him honour reverence and venerate him as the end of the sutta
commentary:
so you see this last partner the buddha is a bit sarcastic he don't venerate a elder monk because of his broken teeth his gray hairs his wrinkles skin but because he's worthy of respect so you see this this this gives you an insight now into life during the time of the buddha sometimes you have these little problems in a community of manga where you have a little troublemaker like this in the suttas there are few udynes and quite a number of udains some 1 or 2 are quite respectable and then 1 or 2 seem to be troublemakers
